message:
perf(pager): zero-frame fast-path in SimplePager::checkpoint

When `pager.checkpoint()` is called on a WAL that has zero frames —
common right after a successful `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, on
a freshly-opened database, or under explicit-PRAGMA callers on an
idle pager — the existing code still paid the full serialization
overhead of a real checkpoint:

- Mark `inner.checkpoint_active = true`, publish pre-checkpoint
  metadata via `publish_metadata_only` (notifying every reader).
- Take `SharedWalBackend.write()` exclusively and REMOVE the backend
  box from the shared state (blocking every concurrent reader and
  writer on that RwLock for the duration).
- Construct a `CheckpointGuard` whose `Drop` re-installs the backend
  + re-publishes `checkpoint_active = false`.

All of that, on a WAL the planner would immediately see has
`frames_to_backfill = 0` and whose post-action would collapse to
`None` (gated on `total_frames > 0` in `plan_checkpoint`), producing
exactly the zero-work `CheckpointResult` we now short-circuit to.

Insert the fast-path AFTER the existing `active_transactions > 0` /
`checkpoint_active` BUSY checks (preserving the contract exercised
by `test_checkpoint_busy_with_active_{reader,writer}`), but BEFORE
the `checkpoint_active = true` assignment and the
`wal_backend.write()` extraction. The probe uses the shared read-
lock helper `with_wal_backend_read` which is the same helper
`wal_frame_count()` uses since 4c71d3b6 — concurrent commits
probing `frame_count` can happen in parallel without blocking.

Also: bump `FailingCheckpointWalBackend::frame_count` fixture from 0
to 1 so its owning test
(`test_checkpoint_coordination_preserves_foreground_isolation_on_checkpoint_error`)
still exercises the backend's failing `checkpoint()` method — the
fast path would otherwise short-circuit before reaching it.

Attribution:
- `maybe_run_adaptive_autocheckpoint` already early-exits when
  `wal_frames_estimate < adaptive_target`, so most post-commit
  advisor calls never land here. Two remaining cases DO:
    - Explicit `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint[(mode)]` from user code.
    - Post-truncate advisor re-entry: a successful checkpoint just
      reset the WAL, next commit's advisor sees frames_estimate = 0
      but `wal_autocheckpoint_pages` is also 0 for a breath,
      causing a brief window where the old code entered checkpoint
      anyway.
- Under MT-writer workloads, each pre-fix `pager.checkpoint()` on
  an empty WAL took `wal_backend.write()` exclusively for the
  duration of the whole publish + guard dance — a serialization
  bubble for every concurrent commit on this pager.

Bench: (no mt_mvcc_bench number — concurrent sibling-agent edits to
`fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` and `fsqlite-mvcc/src/*.rs` were
breaking `cargo check -p fsqlite-core` at capture time. The
behaviour being exercised is structural: before, every zero-frame
checkpoint took `inner.lock()` + `wal_backend.write()` + two
metadata publishes; after, it takes only `inner.lock()` briefly and
a single shared `wal_backend.read()` probe, returning with the same
`CheckpointResult` the slow path would have produced.)

Verification:
- `cargo check -p fsqlite-pager` clean
- `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager --lib -- -D warnings` clean
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-pager test_checkpoint` — 8/8 pass:
    - Pre-existing contract tests all still pass
      (`test_checkpoint_busy_with_active_reader`,
       `test_checkpoint_busy_with_active_writer`,
       `test_checkpoint_coordination_{blocks_new_writer,reports_idle_queue_on_success,preserves_foreground_isolation_on_checkpoint_error}`,
       `test_checkpoint_writer_sync_repairs_page1_header_after_late_growth`,
       `traits::tests::test_checkpoint_page_writer_signatures`).
    - New gate
      `test_checkpoint_empty_wal_fast_path_does_not_extract_backend`
      asserts the fast path (a) returns correct CheckpointResult
      across all four modes (Passive / Full / Restart / Truncate),
      and (b) leaves the SharedWalBackend's mounted backend intact
      so a concurrent `with_wal_backend_read` sees it right after.

Bead: bd-sfdte

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
